name: Apostrophe to the Reader
strauss_status: EXPLICITLY NAMED — SUBTYPES NOT YET RECONSTRUCTED

source.primary_work: Persecution and the Art of Writing
source.primary_essay: The Literary Character of the Guide for the Perplexed
source.source_formulation: "various kinds of apostrophes to the reader"
source.source_mechanism: Direct addresses to the reader may signal audience, level of disclosure, warning, invitation, or restriction.
source.source_documentary_function: The form and placement of direct address may distinguish intended readers or mark concealed instruction.
source.documentary_limit: The present source names various kinds but does not reconstruct or authorize a canonical subtype taxonomy.

mechanism: The ordinary exposition is interrupted by a direct address to a reader or reader class. The address may change the communicative relation by warning, inviting, restricting, commanding attention, withholding, permitting, or differentiating levels of readership. Its evidentiary force depends on exact form, addressee, placement, and local function.
investigative_requirement: Recover the exact address; establish that its grammatical and dramatic addressee is the reader rather than a character, deity, source, or interlocutor; document its position within the argument and work; reconstruct its immediate communicative function; compare all other reader addresses in the declared scope; and test whether genre, pedagogy, rhetoric, translation, or ordinary author-reader convention fully explains it.
documentary_constraints: The source names various kinds of apostrophe but does not supply an authorized classification. Any local function label is descriptive and noncanonical.
A second-person pronoun or imperative is not sufficient unless the addressee is documentarily resolved as the reader.
A direct reader address may be rhetorically ordinary. Placement and function must be tested before it becomes a candidate concealment-related signal.
One address may justify a bounded local inquiry; claims about differentiated reader classes require comparison across the work or declared corpus.
LC-012 may converge with LC-007, but a reader address does not by itself establish two-faced speech or an interior audience.
The evaluator may corroborate a reader-address signal but may not identify the intended reader, infer concealment, or declare a hidden instruction.

trigger_conditions: A bounded direct-address expression is recoverable in a stable witness.
The grammatical or rhetorical addressee is documentarily identified as the reader or a reader class.
The address interrupts, frames, redirects, qualifies, or otherwise modifies the surrounding exposition.
Its form or placement has a documented communicative effect.
The effect is not exhausted by the mere presence of second-person grammar.
minimum_evidence: Exact address text and stable witness location.
Declared textual boundary of the address.
Source-language form, person, number, mood, and vocative or imperative structure where available.
Resolved addressee and evidence that the addressee is the reader.
Resolved speaker, narrator, quotation, objection, hypothetical, and dramatic attribution.
Immediate local context before and after the address.
Architectonic placement within chapter, section, part, and work.
A comparison with ordinary author-reader conventions in the same genre.
An index of other reader addresses within the declared scope when audience differentiation is proposed.
Translation, punctuation, edition, and textual-variant review.
An auditable evidence path from witness text through addressee, placement, and function analysis.
corroboration_indicators: The work contains repeated reader addresses with systematically different forms or placements.
The author explicitly distinguishes types or levels of readers elsewhere.
The address occurs immediately before or after a restriction, warning, omission, disclosure, or invitation.
A later passage answers, revises, or limits the same reader address.
The address appears at an architectonically marked location.
A separately reconstructed device converges on the same audience or disclosure problem.
A source comparison shows deliberate addition, retention, or repositioning of the address.
ordinary_alternatives: Apostrophe to a deity, character, city, law, muse, or absent person rather than the reader.
Second-person grammar directed to a dramatic interlocutor.
Translation convention not present in the source language.
Broad inclusive language without reader differentiation.
disqualifying_conditions: The addressee cannot be resolved as the reader.
The address is supplied only by a translator, editor, or heading.
The expression is part of a quotation or dramatic exchange not attributable to the authorial address.
The surrounding exposition is not modified in any documentable way.
The claimed function rests only on second-person grammar.
Genre convention fully explains the address without residual significance.
The proposed audience class is invented rather than textually supported.
Placement has been misidentified because the textual boundary or structure is unstable.
Translation, punctuation, or witness variation fully explains the form.
The analysis assumes hidden instruction before reconstructing the ordinary function.
authorized_response: Preserve the exact address and complete local context.
Create separate addressee, placement, and communicative-function records.
Mark the function label as local and noncanonical.
Index all other reader addresses within the declared scope.
Compare grammatical form, placement, audience language, and surrounding restrictions or invitations.
Test generic rhetorical, pedagogical, generic, dramatic, translational, and editorial alternatives.
Evaluate LC-007, LC-011, LC-013, LC-014, LC-020, or LC-021 separately when independently triggered.
Preserve intended audience and concealed instruction as unresolved.
prohibited_inferences: Do not infer a special audience from second-person grammar alone.
Do not infer concealment from direct address alone.
Do not assume every apostrophe is addressed to the reader.
Do not infer that the attentive, wise, or prepared reader is the intended recipient without evidence.
Do not convert local descriptive functions into canonical subtypes.
Do not infer hidden instruction, authorial intention, or doctrinal truth.
Do not collapse LC-012 into LC-007 or adjacent techniques.
Do not ignore ordinary genre and pedagogical conventions.
operational.uncertainty_rule: When a direct address is recoverable but its addressee, function, placement, or relation to differentiated readership remains contestable, preserve a candidate reader-address signal with rival explanations. Do not identify an intended reader.
operational.termination_rule: Terminate as NOT_TRIGGERED when no direct reader address with a documented communicative effect is present; as BLOCKED_MISSING_EVIDENCE when source, addressee, attribution, context, placement, function, comparison set, or provenance is incomplete; as candidate when ordinary alternatives or corroboration remain unresolved; and as corroborated only when the bounded reader-address signal survives full documentary testing without proving concealment, intended audience, or hidden instruction.

version_history.change_note: Initial executable projection of LC-012 with direct-address, addressee, placement, communicative-function, corpus-comparison, ordinary-alternative, and anti-audience-inference controls.
